page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton2 processor using the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating at 2.5 GHz, it features 8 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is bundled, featuring a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network performance is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5299.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 12131.32. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 472.98 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 121.9 GB/sec for large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 909.0 and poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds. The instance performs reliably in database operations, achieving 9.83 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and an average Passmark database score of 5244.09.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture combined with local NVMe storage offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space without the premium of separate block storage. The instance is suitable for application servers, static web serving, caching layers, and database workloads that benefit from local SSD storage and balanced memory-to-CPU ratios. It is less optimal for single-threaded compute-heavy tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.2xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5299.0 and strong memory read bandwidth of up to 472.98 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as indicated by a Geekbench single-core score of 909.0. The instance is efficient for workloads requiring balanced compute and fast local storage, such as database operations, caching layers, and static web serving, but is less optimal for intensive single-threaded tasks or machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 Graviton physical cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering balanced multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 8 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
474 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Average multi-core performance with 5299.0 Geekbench multi-core score
Strong database capabilities with 9.83 million Redis SET operations per second
Suitable for web serving, caching, and local-storage-dependent databases
